message:
perf: close performance gap with ast-grep via kind pruning, pattern caching, and binary serialization

Compiled match path narrowed from 14-17x slower to 1.2-1.9x vs ast-grep (native NAPI).
Rule engine now matches or beats ast-grep on small inputs (cs 1.2x faster).

- Add kind-based pruning in searchMatches/searchMatchesInRange (Zig): extract
  pattern root node kind and skip matchNode for non-matching source nodes,
  eliminating 70-90% of work for specific patterns like eval($X)
- Add pattern caching in JS Scanner: compiled pattern handles are reused
  across match()/find()/findAll()/matches() calls via Map<string, number>,
  avoiding repeated tree-sitter parse + WASM alloc per call
- Replace JSON match serialization with binary protocol: packed little-endian
  u32 structs read via DataView, eliminating TextDecoder + JSON.parse overhead
- Add benchmark suite (bench/compare.ts) with ast-grep head-to-head comparison
  using proper warmup (500 iterations) for steady-state measurement

files.patch:
@@ -48,6 +48,8 @@

     "build:js": "bun build src/js/index.ts src/js/cli.ts src/js/types.ts src/js/encoder.ts --outdir dist --target node --format esm --minify --sourcemap=external --splitting && bun x tsc --emitDeclarationOnly",
     "build": "bun run build:zig && bun run build:opt && bun run build:embed && bun run build:js",
+    "bench": "bun bench/index.ts",
+    "bench:compare": "bun bench/compare.ts",
     "test": "bun test",
     "test:zig": "zig build test",
     "prepublishOnly": "bun run build"
